brand.copyright: © 2025 KUM Services GmbH · All Rights Reserved

slides.description: KUM Services GmbH converts proven civil aircraft into customized platforms—delivering performance, reliability, and value faster than traditional programs.
Civil-registered platforms can ferry to your location and convert on-site—minimizing approvals, delays, and total time-to-operation.
From ISR and surveillance to cargo delivery and counter-UAS—configure the airframe to match mission needs, not the other way around.

approach.description: We take the world's most trusted civil aircraft and transform them into mission-specific platforms — combining thousands of hours of proven reliability with the exact capabilities you need, at a fraction of the cost of purpose-built alternatives.

whyUs.lead: Off-the-shelf solutions rarely satisfy demanding operational requirements. Bespoke platforms built on proven civil airframes deliver superior value, faster deployment, and genuine operational fit.

cards.title: Established Reliability
cards.description: Every platform we convert has accumulated thousands of verified flight hours. You inherit decades of engineering validation, approved designs, and a globally trusted maintenance ecosystem from day one.

cards.title: Rapid Time-to-Operation
cards.description: Aircraft retain civil registration and can be ferried directly to your location by pilots. The conversion process takes just one to two days on-site — no special permits, no lengthy approvals, no delays.

cards.title: Significant Cost Savings
cards.description: Eliminating ground-up design and development slashes program costs dramatically. Worldwide parts availability and accessible technical personnel ensure long-term operating costs remain competitive.

cards.title: Operational Flexibility
cards.description: Our approach is not limited to any single airframe or mission type. From light single-engine turboprops to heavy transports, we identify and configure the right platform for your precise requirements.

callout.description: Converted platforms maintain the external appearance of standard civil aircraft. They remain on civil registers, can be operated worldwide with standard flight procedures, and require no special authority clearance for ferry flights — providing significant operational advantage.

aircraft.lead: Each platform is selected based on its operational suitability, global availability, and proven track record. We match the airframe to your mission — not the other way around.

aircrafts.name: Cessna Citation 525B
aircrafts.description: Exceptional speed and altitude capability make the Citation 525B ideal for high-speed, long-range operations. Its jet performance delivers wide-area coverage and rapid response times — a proven platform with a global support network.
details: Best suited for fast turnarounds and mission profiles requiring high cruise performance.
Conversion keeps the civil register and enables worldwide operational flexibility.
We tailor mission systems to your payload, endurance, and installation constraints.

aircrafts.name: Cessna 208
aircrafts.description: A rugged workhorse with strong short-field performance and flexible interior configuration. The 208 is ideal for logistics, ISR payloads, and operations into austere environments.
details: Optimized for multi-role configurations and payload integration.
Suitable for missions that require frequent routing changes and quick deployment.
We define interfaces for sensors, mission computers, and power distribution.

aircrafts.name: C-130J Hercules
aircrafts.description: Maximum range and payload capacity in a single platform. The C-130J conversion represents the pinnacle of large-scale customized aviation capability — suited to the most demanding long-range, high-payload requirements.
details: Designed for high-capacity cargo and mission systems integration.
We support end-to-end project planning with clear milestones.
Payload and mission installation are engineered around your operating environment.

aircrafts.name: Beech King Air 350
aircrafts.description: A highly capable and widely supported multi-mission platform with excellent endurance. The King Air 350 is a proven choice for ISR, special missions, and multi-role configurations.
details: A flexible platform for sensors, communications, and operational mission kits.
We engineer mission integration to support predictable performance and maintenance.
A strong option when you need endurance plus operational versatility.

applications.lead: Our customized platforms serve a broad spectrum of operational roles, each tailored to the precise requirements of the customer — from ISR to long-range cargo delivery.

cards.title: Surveillance & ISR
cards.description: Integrated camera and multi-sensor systems for intelligence gathering, border monitoring, maritime patrol, and persistent area surveillance over extended missions.

cards.title: Cargo & Delivery
cards.description: Transportation and precision delivery of materials and equipment, including parachute-assisted airdrop for austere or inaccessible locations.

cards.title: Defensive Operations
cards.description: Platform configuration for deploying countermeasures, electronic warfare payloads, and protective system packages for defensive mission sets.

cards.title: Range Extension
cards.description: Configured as an airborne range extension and launch platform for cruise and standoff weapon systems, increasing effective strike radius significantly.

cards.title: Attritable Platforms
cards.description: Low-cost, high-availability platforms configured for expendable missions where loss of the aircraft is an acceptable operational outcome.

cards.title: Counter-UAS
cards.description: Detection, tracking, and neutralization of hostile unmanned aerial systems using on-board sensor and effector integration.

partners.lead: Our strength lies in a carefully assembled network of certified technical partners, academic institutions, and aviation specialists — ensuring every platform meets the highest engineering and regulatory standards.

items.label: Certified Maintenance & Repair Organisation
items.title: Part One-Forty Five GmbH
items.role: EASA Part-145 Certified · Neuhausen ob Eck Airfield · Patrick Köpfer
items.text: An authorized EASA PART-145 maintenance and repair organisation specializing in turbine-powered turboprops and jets. Provides the certified technical backbone for all conversion, maintenance, and airworthiness activities across the programme.

items.label: Academic Partner
items.title: University of Stuttgart
items.role: Institute of Flight Mechanics & Control (iFR) · Prof. Dr. Walter Fichter
items.text: The iFR specializes in guidance, control, and navigation of aerospace vehicles. Their Cirrus SR22 (D-EXPT) serves as the autonomous flight demonstration platform, bridging academic research with real-world application.

items.label: Lead Integrator
items.title: KUM Services GmbH
items.text: Prime contractor responsible for end-to-end project management, system integration, and customer delivery. Located at Haltnauer Weg 17, D-78464 Konstanz. Direct contact: +49 172 387 5262 · info@kum-trading.consulting

contact.lead: Describe your operational needs and we will prepare a tailored aircraft recommendation with full technical specification and commercial terms.

form.heading: Send an Enquiry
form.sub: All enquiries are handled with strict confidentiality. We typically respond within one business day.
form.note: Your information is treated with complete confidentiality and will not be shared with third parties.
